author: qu0b
content: Whats the point of supporting multiple proof types in a single kurtosis network? Are validators expected to validate different proof types?

author: qu0b
content: Shouldn’t each kurtosis invocation have one global proof config?

author: barnabasbusa
content:
did you guys change something lately in ere? https://github.com/ethpandaops/eth-client-docker-image-builder/actions/runs/25255900272/job/74055167748#step:4:1 

imgs aren’t building anymore

author: taulepton_
content: yes they are. the idea is that we have proof diversity, you can consider this similar to client diversity.

author: taulepton_
content: There may have been some changes related to Hans PR above but I'm not certain of this

author: barnabasbusa
content:
https://github.com/ethpandaops/eth-client-docker-image-builder/blob/master/ere-server-zisk/build.sh

this is the build script we are using

author: barnabasbusa
content: i can see if any updates are required

author: taulepton_
content: I would defer to Han on this I’m not aware. Maybe the ere repo would shed some light

author: qu0b
content: Are these just dummy proofs? Or how is a local multi proof setup realised?

author: taulepton_
content: Both dummy and gpu. There is one service / container per zkvm prover (ere server) with gpus assigned to each service. Zkboost service acts as a middleware between client <-> ere provers with standardised interface

author: taulepton_
content: For dummy there is no ere service and zkboost produces dummy proofs itself

author: qu0b
content: So with 1 GPU 5090 i can run multiple prover backends?

author: taulepton_
content: I don’t think you can do partial assignment of gpu to a prover backends. You would need n gpus to serve n prover backends at the minimum. In practice if you want real time proving you will likely need more than 1 gpu per prover backend

author: taulepton_
content: I may be wrong about partial assignment as I haven’t tested it. However in practice I doubt we’d use it unless proof gen gets efficient enough to not require multiple gpus per prover backed

author: taulepton_
content: It should be noted that we want to produce different proof types in parallel

author: qu0b
content: So for locally testing multiple proof types I would need multiple GPUs. At that point id rather have proving as a service than running it locally

author: taulepton_
content: It’s likely proof providers will emerge. For those that want to operate their own infra they can do. Providers can censor, having an open source solution with easy deployment addresses the censor concern

author: taulepton_
content: Why do you want proving as a service for testing?

author: qu0b
content: I just want to better understand what the expectations are for clients that want to run this as we can’t expect everyone to have multiple GPUs to run a network locally but we can very well assume one GPU and we should focus not only on optimizing for our personal case but also keep in mind to make it as accessible as possible to others for testing
